title: Cat Head Tilt to the Right: Causes, Urgency, and Care Guide
meta_desc: Is your cat's head tilted to the right? Learn about vestibular issues, when to see a vet, and how to help your feline friend recover.
content_html:
What is a Persistent Head Tilt?

A persistent head tilt, such as your cat constantly leaning their head to the right, is typically a clinical sign of vestibular dysfunction. The vestibular system is a complex network responsible for maintaining balance and spatial orientation. When there is a disturbance in this system—either in the inner ear (peripheral) or the brain (central)—your cat loses their sense of where the ground is, resulting in a tilted posture as they attempt to compensate for the sensation of spinning.

Is This an Emergency?

The urgency level for a head tilt is Medium. While a head tilt is rarely an immediate life-threatening emergency on its own, it is a sign of an underlying medical issue that needs to be addressed by a veterinarian, ideally within 24 hours. You should upgrade this to an emergency if the head tilt is accompanied by a complete inability to walk, falling over, or rhythmic, uncontrolled eye flickering. Most cases are caused by treatable issues like ear infections or idiopathic vestibular disease, but prompt diagnosis is key to comfort.

How Photos and Videos Help Triage

Capturing a photo or, more importantly, a short video of your cat's head tilt is incredibly valuable for your veterinarian. Cats are notorious for masking symptoms or acting differently due to the stress of a clinic visit. A video taken in a calm, home environment allows the vet to see the exact degree of the tilt and observe any subtle eye movements or gait abnormalities that might not be visible during the physical exam. This helps differentiate between an ear issue and a neurological concern more quickly.

faqs.question: What are the most common causes of head tilts in cats?
faqs.answer: The most common causes include middle or inner ear infections, inflammatory polyps, or 'old cat' vestibular disease (idiopathic vestibular syndrome). Less common causes include toxins or neurological issues.

faqs.question: Can a cat recover from a head tilt?
faqs.answer: Yes, many cats make a full recovery once the underlying cause, such as an infection, is treated. In some cases, a slight residual tilt may remain even after the cat has regained its balance and normal activity levels.

faqs.question: Should I try to clean my cat's ears at home if I see a tilt?
faqs.answer: No, do not put any cleaners or medications into the ear before a vet exam. If the eardrum is ruptured, certain cleaners can actually make the vestibular symptoms worse or cause permanent damage.
